## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 21, 1857, the incredibly massive, historically devastating, and deeply symbolic fall of Delhi aggressively occurred. Following an incredibly brutal, massive months-long siege during the Indian Rebellion, the heavily armed British East India Company forces aggressively recaptured the capital. They forcefully and aggressively apprehended the incredibly massive, elderly Mughal Emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar at Humayun's Tomb. His absolute, highly humiliating surrender and subsequent exile to Rangoon fundamentally and permanently annihilated the massive, three-century-old Mughal Empire.

On September 21, 1743, Sawai Jai Singh II, the incredibly massive, fiercely brilliant, and universally revered Rajput ruler of the Kingdom of Amber, officially passed away.
